h2h_summary: The two nations have met five times since 1991, splitting the record evenly at two wins apiece and one draw, with both teams winning 2-1 across multiple encounters including a recent June 2025 friendly win for Türkiye.
wc_history_home: Türkiye's finest World Cup moment came in 2002, when they finished third — their only other appearance being the 1954 group stage — with Hakan Şükür scoring the fastest goal in World Cup history (10.8 seconds) in the third-place match against South Korea.
wc_history_away: The USMNT are appearing in their 12th World Cup and their best result in the modern era was a quarter-final finish in 2002, while their best ever result was a semi-final in the inaugural 1930 tournament; as co-hosts in 2026 they face immense pressure to equal or surpass that 2002 peak.
group_context: Group D features co-hosts USA alongside Türkiye, Paraguay, and Australia; the USA enter as strong favourites on home soil and need at least one win to advance, while Türkiye — returning to the World Cup for the first time since 2002 — require a result here to keep their knockout stage hopes alive after opening games.
strengths_home: Elite creative duo in Güler and Yıldız capable of unlocking any defence with pace, skill, and vision
Çalhanoğlu's leadership, passing range, and set-piece delivery give Türkiye midfield control
Strong recent form — four wins in last four matches including a 4-0 friendly over North Macedonia and 2-1 win over USA in 2025
strengths_away: Home crowd advantage at SoFi Stadium provides a significant psychological and atmospheric edge
Deep European-based squad with elite club experience through Pulisic, McKennie, Adams, and Balogun
High-energy pressing system under Pochettino creates turnovers and generates attacking transitions
team_style_home: Türkiye under Vincenzo Montella play a fluid 4-2-3-1 built on possession control and high pressing, using the creativity of Arda Güler and Kenan Yıldız to break down defences while Çalhanoğlu provides control and leadership from deep.
team_style_away: The USMNT under Mauricio Pochettino employ a high-pressing 3-4-2-1/3-2-5 hybrid that shifts to a 4-2-3-1 defensively, using wing-backs for width and relying on Pulisic's movement and Balogun's finishing to create and convert chances.
recent_news: The United States Men’s National Team faces a lineup dilemma ahead of their World Cup Group D finale against Turkey, with injuries to Cristian Roldan and Christian Pulisic. Turkey have no injuries to contend with heading into the match.
key_players_home: Hakan Çalhanoğlu (captain, deep-lying playmaker, Inter Milan)
Arda Güler (attacking midfielder / playmaker, Real Madrid)
context: Group D features co-hosts USA alongside Türkiye, Paraguay, and Australia; the USA enter as strong favourites on home soil and need at least one win to advance, while Türkiye — returning to the World Cup for the first time since 2002 — require a result here to keep their knockout stage hopes alive after opening games.
